title: narcissus tazetta subsp. cypri
intro: Narcissus tazetta subsp. cypri, also known as the Cypriot daffodil or wild daffodil, is a subspecies of the N. tazetta plant, which is a small, perennial bulbous plant that blooms with clusters of small, white or yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of N. tazetta subsp. cypri include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the flowers or bulbs in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
